title: Lesson 28 — Peril and Rescue

words.example: The climbers found a precarious foothold on the ledge.
They made a perilous crossing over the flooded river.
One careless leak could imperil the whole mission.

words.definition: to put at risk of loss, harm, or failure
words.example: A single scandal could jeopardize the merger.
Icy roads make the mountain pass hazardous in winter.

words.definition: a dangerous, difficult, or unfortunate situation
words.example: The documentary drew attention to the refugees' plight.

words.definition: a difficult, unpleasant, or embarrassing situation
words.example: Missing the last train left us in a real predicament.

words.definition: a state of uncertainty over what to do; a dilemma
words.example: She was in a quandary over which offer to accept.

words.definition: to free from a difficulty or entanglement
words.example: It took months to extricate the company from the lawsuit.

words.definition: to rescue or save from loss or destruction
words.example: They managed to salvage a few files from the crash.

words.definition: a temporary relief from danger or trouble
words.example: The rain gave the firefighters a brief reprieve.
Without aid, the situation would soon become dire.

story: The expedition's situation had turned [[dire|dire]]. A [[precarious|precarious]] snow bridge offered the only route across, and the [[perilous|perilous]] weather threatened to [[imperil|imperil]] everyone on the rope. One wrong step would [[jeopardize|jeopardize]] the whole team; the descent below was equally [[hazardous|hazardous]]. Their guide understood the [[plight|plight]] at once, but the [[predicament|predicament]] left her in a genuine [[quandary|quandary]]: press on or turn back. She chose to [[extricate|extricate]] the group one climber at a time, hoping to [[salvage|salvage]] the attempt, and when the clouds briefly parted, that [[reprieve|reprieve]] was just enough to get them all down alive.
